title: ISE ANC Closed-Loop Effectiveness — Quarantine-to-Compromise-Stop Time

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.17 ICT-related incident management — containment metrics.
compliance.controlObjective: Containment-effectiveness KPI for adaptive-network-control quarantine actions.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.26 response to information security incidents.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Quarantine an endpoint actively attacking. Measure time until malicious traffic stops. Should be <2 minutes; otherwise UC-17.1.80 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Quarantine immediately stops attack. Healthy KPI.

dataSources: ISE ANC events `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` MessageCode 86001 (ANC-applied) — combined with ES notable events `index=notable` and firewall/IDS data showing whether the endpoint's malicious traffic stopped after ANC. Time-window correlation: ANC apply time vs. last malicious event time per endpoint MAC.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` + Splunk Enterprise Security.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=86001 anc_action=quarantine
| eval anc_time=_time, endpoint_mac=mac
| join endpoint_mac [search index=notable severity=high earliest=-1d | rename _time as last_attack_time]
| eval quarantine_to_attack_stop=anc_time - last_attack_time
| stats avg(quarantine_to_attack_stop) as avg_seconds, count by anc_policy
description: Measures whether ANC quarantine actually stops the malicious activity. Correlates ANC timestamp with the last observed malicious event — if attacks continue post-ANC, the closed loop is broken.
value: ANC is only as good as the network enforcement that follows. If switches don't enforce CoA, malicious activity continues — ANC becomes theatre. This UC measures the actual security outcome, not just the ANC API call.
visualization: Quarantine-to-stop time gauge; per-endpoint timeline; failed-quarantine list.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Endpoint moved to a different switch port — CoA didn't propagate. Operations issue.
(b) Endpoint legitimately back on network after admin unquarantine.
mitreAttack: N/A (response-loop telemetry — measures ANC closed-loop quarantine flow, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: ISE can quarantine a misbehaving device. We measure if the bad activity actually stops after quarantine — sometimes the quarantine doesn't take effect, and bad traffic continues.
